lyrics:
You said you just need time
Packed your books in that blue tote bag
Left your sweater on my chair
Said
“I’ll call you in a week
Maybe”

I watched you lock the door
Every footstep down the hall felt slow
Coffee going cold in my hand
Trying not to beg you to stay

I’m counting every hour since you left
Talking to the ceiling in our bed
I’m halfway through a message I won’t send
I miss you like you wouldn’t believe
But you said
“Not long enough
For me
Not long enough
For me”

Your toothbrush still by the sink
I move it
Then I move it back
Every song in this old car
Hits a little harder
Makes me crack

Friends say
“Give it space”
But this apartment hums with your name
I scroll through photos
Stop and stare
You’re laughing in the sunlight
Out of frame

I’m counting every hour since you left
Talking to the ceiling in our bed
I’m halfway through a message I won’t send
I miss you like you wouldn’t believe
But you said
“Not long enough
For me
Not long enough
For me”

Then one day I slept right in
Didn’t wake up reaching for your side
Met a stranger at a friend’s place
She talked too fast
But her eyes were kind

We got lost walking home
Shared a jacket when the rain came down (oh)
Laughed about our broken hearts
Felt the weight start lifting off the ground

Phone lights up
It’s you
“Think I’m ready
Can we hit rewind?”
You said
“I finally figured us out
I’m done running from what we had inside”

But I’ve been breathing easy
Finally
Found a little peace where you left me
There’s a hand in mine that feels like home this week
You had all the time you’d ever need
Now I say
“Not long enough
For me
Not long enough
For me” (no, no)

Funny how the pieces fall
How the hardest calls
Turn into the right directions

All that hurt
All that wait
Was a doorway
Not a cage
Sometimes losing’s just protection

We were chapters
Not the book
Just a place I had to look
To learn what I was meant to be

I’m not counting every hour anymore
Got my feet back on the floor
What I begged you for is something I don’t need
You had all the time you’d ever need
Now I say
“Not long enough
For me
Not long enough
For me”
I’m right where I’m supposed to be
Not long enough
For me
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track opens with a clean, spacious texture, creating an atmosphere of groovy, reflective melancholy. The energy is a steady, mid-level funk feel, driven by a tight, clear rhythm section and punctuated by soulful vocal ad-libs and horn stabs. The production is polished and balanced, with each element occupying its own space, from the prominent bassline to the layered background vocals, creating a dense yet uncluttered mix. The song's defining quality is its narrative arc: a distinct musical and lyrical shift halfway through transforms the track from a story of heartbreak into a triumphant anthem of moving on, cleverly recontextualizing the main hook.

review.review_text: PCBender turns a breakup song inside out here, and the twist lands because the lyric earns it: the same chorus that opens as a wound, 'not long enough for me,' closes as a door slammed shut on the narrator's terms. The track moves at a steady 107 BPM in F# minor, grounded by a bassline that gives the groove more funk than the blues-rock tags suggest, and the production is clean enough that every shift in the story registers clearly. The real craft is structural: by the time a stranger shows up sharing a jacket in the rain, you feel the emotional center of gravity actually move, which is harder to pull off than it sounds. A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric: 'not long enough for me' recontextualized at close
lyric: 'halfway through a message I won't send'
narrative structural shift at the stranger/rain section
